explanatory_strength.rationale: The levered-treasury mechanism (ETH look-through versus ATM dilution and corporate burn) is specific, but the financing-cycle stress path is not separated cleanly from pure ETH beta.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Filings anchor ETH units, share count, cash, and deferred tax, yet BMNP preferred claims, post-May ATM pace, and a full senior-claim waterfall remain unresolved for credit stress.

downside_control.rationale: May 2026 ETH fair value per share (~$18.76) offers a partial floor, but unbounded equity issuance, $287.6M nine-month operating cash use, and G&A far above staking revenue can overwhelm look-through value.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Base annualized return is 6.3% per year, below the 12% credit hurdle; the bull case (~15.1%) requires limited further dilution and ETH re-rating.

claims.claim: BMNR carries no material term debt, but the equity-financing cycle is the dominant fixed-claim risk: shares outstanding rose to 579.7M from 232.4M in one year while nine-month operating cash use was $287.6M against $340.3M cash.

The warrants, deferred tax, and preferred-claim reserve is modeled at zero in base case despite $92.3M deferred tax liability and unresolved BMNP preferred stock; credit stress can absorb ETH look-through before common equity realizes upside.

Component overlap controls appear clean (static ETH look-through separated from trajectory net of dilution), but the eth_trajectory row lacks explicit remaining issuance capital and probability, so the low case ($8.5/sh total, -45.8% to price) is not a fully reconciled financing-cycle floor.

strongest_counter_explanation: Price at $15.69 sits below May 2026 ETH fair value per share (~$18.76); if ATM issuance pauses and ETH appreciates, mNAV could normalize without a refinancing or insolvency event because the company holds $340.3M cash and no operating debt at scale.
most_important_missing_fact: BMNP preferred stock conversion terms and post-May 2026 ATM dilution pace reconciled to net ETH per diluted common share after all senior claims.
falsifiers: Reconcile preferred, warrant, and deferred-tax claims into a per-share waterfall that nets against ETH look-through.
Track two consecutive quarters where ATM issuance dilutes ETH per share faster than token price rises.
Model corporate G&A and operating cash burn against staking inflow under a no-new-equity stress case.
specialist_findings: Credit cross-check: survivability near term looks supported by cash and absent term maturities, but the equity stub is exposed to an open-ended issuance cycle and an under-modeled senior-claim stack; defer until the preferred waterfall and dilution path are evidenced.
